text.title: Growth Affiliate Marketing Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Growth Affiliate Marketing` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Total commission per acquired customer MUST be <= 25% of LTV in the first 12 months. Recurring tiers allowed but capped at 30% lifetime.
rule.rationale: Commissions above the cap turn affiliate channel into a worse-than-paid-ads channel — high CAC + share of LTV. Cap forces deliberate tradeoff vs. paid.

rule.statement: Cookie attribution window MUST be 30-90 days. Shorter under-rewards consideration cycles; longer creates over-attribution + revenue dispute.

rationale.source: affiliate-platform default settings; partner agreement reviews

rule.rationale: 30 days catches direct-response sales; 60-90 catches B2B consideration cycles. Outside this range, attribution becomes noise or theft.

rule.statement: Every affiliate MUST agree to FTC disclosure rules in writing (US partners) + ASA/EU equivalents where applicable. Audit >= 10% of placements quarterly.

rationale.source: FTC 16 CFR Part 255; ASA guidance UK 2024

rule.rationale: Undisclosed affiliate content is a regulatory liability AND a trust hit. FTC fines + platform deplatforming risk. Disclosure is the contract; quarterly audit is the proof.

rule.statement: Affiliate recruitment MUST prioritize audience-match over follower count. 10k-engaged > 100k-broadcast for conversion.

rationale.source: PartnerStack benchmarks 2024; Pat Flynn affiliate playbook

rule.rationale: Big-list affiliates send tire-kickers; small-list niche affiliates send buyers. Recruitment criteria: audience overlap + content alignment + engagement rate.

rule.statement: Active fraud detection MUST run (cookie stuffing, self-referrals, brand-keyword bidding). Quarterly affiliate audit + click-pattern monitor.
rule.rationale: Affiliate fraud (cookie stuffing, brand-name bidding, fake conversions) silently leaks 10-25% of program budget. Active detection + auditable cancellation policy.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
